title: Tavita's Touch
intro: Every piece begins as a shapeless lump of clay. What makes it beautiful is one thing — her hands. The brand is built on that single truth: these aren't just ceramics, they're the imprint of her touch.

subject.text:
THE RAW MATERIAL
It starts as nothing — a wet, shapeless lump. Every finished piece is just clay, plus her hands.

difference.text:
THE IDEA
The whole brand rests on one truth: the beauty isn't in the clay, it's in the touch. That's the name, and that's the strategy.

anchor.text:
THE ANCHOR
Tavita is my wife. Watching her work, the idea was obvious — she doesn't make objects, she leaves an imprint. The identity had to be that imprint too.

mark.text:
THE MARK
Two T's meeting like two hands closing around clay. One continuous line — the gesture of a touch, not a drawn letter.

typeColor.text:
TYPE & COLOR
A thin serif and a palette pulled straight from the clay. Restrained, exact — the mark of a hand in full control.

inWorld.text:
IN THE MATERIAL
On every piece, the mark is pressed into the clay — the last, most literal touch of her hand. The brand isn't printed on the work. It's left in it.

system.text:
THE SYSTEM
One idea, carried from the name to the mark to the stamp in the clay. A brand that means one thing, everywhere — and that's what makes it impossible to mistake.
